question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $72,000 on April 20?

fragility_points: A >4% downside move before resolution would likely flip the outcome.
Narrow timestamp mechanics increase path dependence.
key_assumptions: Recent above-72k Binance trading is informative for the next four days.
No major downside shock arrives before April 20 noon ET.
The noon ET minute close is not unusually likely to diverge from the broader spot regime.
main_logical_chain: Check the exact Polymarket rules and named settlement source.
Observe BTC is currently above the threshold on Binance and has recently traded above it repeatedly.
Apply an outside-view volatility haircut because a single-minute settle four days away is materially fragile.
Conclude Yes is favored, but less strongly than the 83.5% market-implied probability suggests.
payload.main_thesis: BTC is already above 72k on the named exchange and recent Binance trading supports a Yes lean, but the market overstates certainty because settlement depends on one specific noon ET minute close four days away.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon)... has a final "Close" price higher than... 72,000.
payload.source_quality_view: Source-of-truth ambiguity is low because Polymarket explicitly names Binance BTC/USDT 1m close at 12:00 ET; evidence independence is medium because the decisive evidence is appropriately concentrated on Binance.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract resolves on a single 12:00 ET one-minute close rather than a daily close or average price.

Only four days remain, so regime persistence matters more than long-range thesis changes.
payload.timing_relevance: This is a short-dated threshold market resolving at April 20, 2026 12:00 ET, so persistence vs near-term volatility dominates.
unresolved_ambiguities: No direct historical noon-ET minute analysis was performed.

payload.what_would_change_view: A sustained drop below 72k on Binance, a new downside shock, or evidence that the noon ET minute is unusually unfavorable would reduce the Yes estimate.
